perturbation.original_text: 4.1 License Fees. In consideration for the licenses granted to Corio pursuant to Section 2 of this Agreement, Corio shall pay the license fees specified in EXHIBIT B hereto. Payment terms of such license fees shall be as set forth in EXHIBIT B hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 License Fees. In consideration for the licenses granted to Corio pursuant to Section 2 of this Agreement, Corio shall pay the license fees specified in EXHIBIT B hereto. Payment terms of such license fees shall be immediate upon the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction. Section 4.1 states that license fees payment terms are outlined in Exhibit B. However, the modified text specifies that payment is due immediately upon the Effective Date, overriding any payment terms potentially outlined in Exhibit B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PAYMENT TERMS License Fees: Due upon Software Acceptance. Maintenance and Support Fees: Net 30 of Software acceptance anniversary date, and each year thereafter. Revenue Sharing Fees: Quarterly payments shall be due to receiving party, net 30 days after quarter close. Implementation or Professional Service Fees: Net 30 days from date of Commerce One invoice, which shall be issued only after successful completion of each agreed upon milestone. MarketSite.net Access Fee: Due upon Software Acceptance, and on each annual anniversary of Software Acceptance thereafter.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 4.1 specifies that payment is due immediately upon the Effective Date, directly conflicting with the Contradicted Text, 'PAYMENT TERMS License Fees: Due upon Software Acceptance'. The original text defers to Exhibit B, so the contradiction arises because the change specifies payment terms that override those stated in the Contradicted Text.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect for an initial period of five (5) years. Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically renew for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party provides the other party with written notification at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the initial five (5) year term or any one (1) year renewal thereof of its intention to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect indefinitely, unless terminated as outlined in section 11.2.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the agreement's duration. The original text states a five-year initial term with one-year renewals, while the modified text specifies an indefinite term unless terminated. This creates ambiguity about the agreement's lifespan and renewal process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Term. The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in full force and effect indefinitely, unless terminated as outlined in section 11.2. Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically renew for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party provides the other party with written notification at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the initial five (5) year term or any one (1) year renewal thereof of its intention to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the agreement continues 'indefinitely, unless terminated as outlined in section 11.2'. The contradicted text includes 'indefinitely' but also states 'Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically renew for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party provides the other party with written notification at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the initial five (5) year term or any one (1) year renewal thereof'. These two clauses contradict each other. The changed text removes the definite term and renewal process, creating an inconsistency regarding the agreement's duration. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the different sections provide conflicting terms about the length of the contract, which leads to uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 12.3 License. Upon the release of the Source Code to Corio pursuant to Section 12.2 of this Agreement, Corio shall have a royalty-free, nonexclusive, nontransferable, right and license in the Territory to use and modify the Source Code to support and maintain the Software until the expiration or termination of Corio's Customers' End User License Agreements. The object code derived from the Source Code so modified shall be subject to the same rights and restrictions on use, reproduction and disclosure that are contained in this Agreement with respect to the Software. Corio shall not distribute, sell or sublicense the Source Code. Subject to the licenses expressly granted in this Agreement, Commerce One shall retain all right, title and interest in and to the Source Code. This license shall be deemed to extend worldwide in scope if Corio, at the time one or more Release Conditions has occurred, has been granted worldwide license rights by Commerce One under Section 2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.3 License. Upon the release of the Source Code to Corio pursuant to Section 12.2 of this Agreement, Corio shall have a royalty-free, exclusive, transferable, right and license in the Territory to use and modify the Source Code to support and maintain the Software perpetually. The object code derived from the Source Code so modified shall be subject to different rights and restrictions on use, reproduction and disclosure that are contained in this Agreement with respect to the Software. Corio will be able to distribute, sell or sublicense the Source Code. Subject to the licenses expressly granted in this Agreement, Commerce One shall retain no right, title and interest in and to the Source Code. This license shall be deemed to extend worldwide in scope if Corio, at the time one or more Release Conditions has occurred, has been granted worldwide license rights by Commerce One under Section 2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates several in-text contradictions. The original section 12.3 states the license is non-exclusive and non-transferable. The modified text makes the license exclusive and transferable, contradicting the original terms. The original also states that Corio shall not distribute, sell, or sublicense the source code, this is changed so Corio will be able to do so. Finally, the original states that Commerce One shall retain all right, title and interest in and to the Source Code, but the modification makes it so Commerce One retains no right, title and interest in and to the Source Code
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.3 License. Upon the release of the Source Code to Corio pursuant to Section 12.2 of this Agreement, Corio shall have a royalty-free, exclusive, transferable, right and license in the Territory to use and modify the Source Code to support and maintain the Software perpetually. The object code derived from the Source Code so modified shall be subject to different rights and restrictions on use, reproduction and disclosure that are contained in this Agreement with respect to the Software. Corio will be able to distribute, sell or sublicense the Source Code. Subject to the licenses expressly granted in this Agreement, Commerce One shall retain no right, title and interest in and to the Source Code. This license shall be deemed to extend worldwide in scope if Corio, at the time one or more Release Conditions has occurred, has been granted worldwide license rights by Commerce One under Section 2 of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several direct contradictions with the original terms as outlined in the explanation. The shift from 'non-exclusive, nontransferable' to 'exclusive, transferable,' the allowance of distribution/sublicensing where it was previously prohibited, and the transfer of ownership from Commerce One to Corio all create clear and irreconcilable conflicts within section 12.3 itself.
